newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The data-center cooling pivot (Q1 +500% DC orders, ZutaCore stake, $1.5B 2026 DC target, Riello divestiture, ~3,000 job cuts) was disclosed with Q1 results earlier in 2026 and reaffirmed at conferences. Stock essentially flat (-0.4%) since June 9.

sources.label: Carrier Q1 beat driven by surging data-center orders
Carrier reshapes portfolio toward data-center cooling and buybacks

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: cooling/thermal exposure is real but Carrier sits farther from the passive/power bottleneck stack and the DC-cooling story is already known. No new evidence June 9-23; flat tape removes the prior crowding penalty's catalyst. Hold rank 57.
verdicts.bull: DC cooling orders surging (+500% Q1); ZutaCore liquid-cooling optionality; portfolio focus on high-margin climate.
verdicts.neutral: Story fully disclosed; megacap (~$59B) limits residual alpha vs passives.
verdicts.bear: AI-cooling exposure diluted in a large diversified HVAC franchise; +33% YTD already rewards the pivot.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical commercial HVAC / diluted AI exposure

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck list: power/cooling/electrical bottleneck thesis intact - DC cooling demand and QuantumLeap/ZutaCore stack remain central - but no new evidence since June 9. Rank 8 balances bottleneck exposure against valuation recognition. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Direct play on data-center thermal bottleneck with $1.5B 2026 DC target and liquid-cooling stack.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis reaffirmed at conferences, no fresh datapoint; rank already reflects recognition.
verdicts.bear: Large-cap dilution of the bottleneck; rerating risk after +33% YTD.
